name: Specifications & Details
description: How technical and product specifications should be presented for informed decision making

heuristics.principle: Technical specifications should be scannable and consistently formatted
checkpoints: Specs are presented in a structured format (table, list)
Labels and values are clearly differentiated
Formatting is consistent across all specification items

heuristics.principle: Terminology should be explained where it may not be universally understood
checkpoints: Technical terms include brief explanations or tooltips
Industry jargon is translated to user-friendly language
Abbreviations are spelled out or explained
Complex specifications include context for interpretation

heuristics.principle: Specification content should be complete and not rely on external documentation
checkpoints: Key specifications are listed on the page
Users do not need to download PDFs for basic info
Dimensions, materials, and key attributes are present
Compatibility information is included where relevant
